data.title: As a DSO user or developer, I can find documentation for every configuration key used by DSO in CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Additions to CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md covering every configuration key identified in the gap audit report's gap list -- keys used in the codebase but not yet documented.
**Why**: CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md exists but is missing key namespaces. Users who try to configure DSO for integrations, advanced features, or CI pipelines will hit undocumented keys with no guidance.
**Scope**:
- IN: Add entries for every key on the gap audit report's gap list with description, type, default value, and example; known gap namespaces from prior analysis listed in Considerations
- OUT: Creating or restructuring CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md (additive only); changes to any other documentation file; documenting integration credentials (Story D)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, every configuration key identified in the gap audit report's gap list is documented in CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md with a description, type, default value, and example
  <- Satisfies: "Every key on the gap list absent from CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md is added with a description before epic closure"

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] Known gap namespaces from prior analysis (gap audit in Story A may surface additional keys): brainstorm.enforce_entry_gate, sprint.max_replan_cycles, test_gate.*, test_quality.*, worktree.isolation_enabled, orchestration.max_agents, design.figma_collaboration, bug_report.title_warning_enabled, ticket_clarity.threshold, clarity_check.pass_threshold
- [Maintainability] New configuration key entries must not alter existing section headings or anchor IDs in CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md -- Story C's INSTALL.md links to specific anchors in this file; changing heading text would break those links

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue -- one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
